title: Parkio Daily: May 22, 2026
teaser: Millennium Falcon gains new Mandalorian missions today, plus a summer preview at Magic Kingdom and Animal Kingdom kicks off next week.

rides.name: Big Thunder Mountain Railroad
rides.note: Freshly reopened after year-long refurb, waits still moderate

rides.name: Buzz Lightyear's Space Ranger Spin
rides.note: Upgraded scoring system draws repeat riders

breaking.title: Millennium Falcon: Smugglers Run debuts Mandalorian missions today
breaking.body: Starting today at Hollywood Studios and Disneyland, Smugglers Run adds new interactive missions featuring Mando and Grogu. Engineers can now tag bounty targets and choose flight paths across Tatooine, with a hidden "Grogu Mode" unlock available for skilled crews.

parkioInsight.text: Star Wars fans tend to pack Galaxy's Edge on attraction debut days. Rope-drop or late-evening windows usually offer shorter waits for the new missions.

bignews.title: Cool Kids Summer experiences preview next week at two parks
bignews.body: Magic Kingdom's Diamond Horseshoe hosts Jessie's Roundup starting May 28, while Animal Kingdom's Conservation Station debuts Bluey's Wild World the same day. Both are part of the summer-long Cool Kids initiative.

parkioInsight.text: Conservation Station is often overlooked and rarely crowded. Bluey's Wild World may draw families with young kids, so visiting in the first hour after opening typically keeps waits light.

bignews.title: Spaceship Earth overhaul plan officially canceled
bignews.body: The previously announced reimagining of EPCOT's icon attraction is no longer moving forward. The ride will continue operating in its current form without the planned narrative and technology updates.

parkioInsight.text: Spaceship Earth's wait times usually drop to walk-on status during evening hours. Worth riding for air conditioning and a rest break after a busy World Showcase loop.

bignews.title: Disneyland hosted LA Fleet Week military celebration yesterday
bignews.body: Over 100 service members from the Navy, Marines, and Coast Guard visited Disneyland on May 21 as part of Disney Celebrates America. The event included a Main Street flag retreat, Navy band performance, and F/A-18 flyover.

parkioInsight.text: Mickey and Minnie debuted new patriotic costumes for the event. These outfits may appear at additional America 250 celebrations through July 4 weekend—worth checking character schedules if you're a costume fan.

topstories.title: Studio Lot reimagining soft-opens at Hollywood Studios
topstories.body: Construction walls came down ahead of the official May 26 debut, revealing refreshed theming and a new Mickey Mouse Clubhouse live show. The updated land includes enhanced placemaking details throughout the former Animation Courtyard area.

parkioInsight.text: Soft openings often mean lighter crowds before official debuts. Checking the new show and details before Memorial Day weekend may offer easier access than waiting until next week.

topstories.title: La Signature shop in France Pavilion sets temporary closure date
topstories.body: The EPCOT World Showcase boutique will close for a brief refurbishment starting in early June. Reopening timing has not been announced.

parkioInsight.text: If you're shopping for specific French goods or Givenchy fragrances, visiting before the closure date likely avoids disappointment. Merchandise selections can shift after refurbs.

topstories.title: Disney World pilots private PhotoPass resort shoots for $399
topstories.body: A new Signature Portrait Sessions program offers 20-minute private photo sessions with PhotoPass photographers at select resorts. The service includes digital downloads and runs as a pilot test.

parkioInsight.text: Resort-based shoots usually offer better lighting and fewer background crowds than in-park sessions. Booking early morning or golden hour slots tends to yield the strongest photo results.

icymi.title: Universal pokes fun at Big Thunder reopening on social media
icymi.body: Universal Orlando posted about its Seuss Trolley ride being "the wildest ride in Seuss Landing," echoing Big Thunder Mountain's tagline just days after Disney's reopening. Theme park fans quickly caught the shade.

spotlight.title: Buzz Lightyear scoring system upgraded after refurb
spotlight.body: Magic Kingdom's Buzz Lightyear's Space Ranger Spin now features improved target detection and higher scoring potential. Guests report more responsive gameplay and easier path to Galactic Hero status after the recent technical refresh.

parkioInsight.text: Tomorrowland tends to see afternoon crowd surges. Riding Buzz Lightyear in the first two operating hours or after 8pm usually keeps lines under 15 minutes and lets you test the new scoring without long waits.
